Title: Landslides kill 15 in Philippines as biggest typhoon in decades nears Taiwan
Article:
KEELUNG, Taiwan: Fifteen people were killed in landslides in the Philippines, and more than 2,000 in Taiwan were evacuated from their homes, as the biggest typhoon in decades neared the region on Friday.
Typhoon Bavi is expected to pound Taiwan's north and east, as well as Japan's remote southwestern islands, on Friday and Saturday before smashing into China, which has been hit by deadly storms this week.
Two landslides triggered by heavy rains driven by Bavi killed at least 15 people and left six others missing on the Philippines' southern island of Mindanao, authorities said.
Locals in Taiwan's port city of Keelung, which is expected to be one of the hardest hit areas on the island, stocked up on food, taped windows, and stacked sandbags along shop fronts, heeding warnings from authorities to take precautions.
"They're saying it's going to be huge; of course that's scary, right?" Keelung grocery store owner Chang Shih-huo, 76, told AFP.
"We've stocked up on some instant noodles and bread and stuff like that. Once the wind and rain really start picking up, we'll have to close the shop."
After hitting Guam and the Northern Marianas on Monday as a super typhoon, Bavi was downgraded to a typhoon as it moved across the Pacific Ocean.
Bavi's maximum sustained wind speeds slowed to 155 kilometres (96 miles) per hour, with gusts of around 190 kilometres per hour, on Friday, the Central Weather Administration (CWA) said.
"The typhoon is likely to continue weakening because environmental conditions are not favourable," CWA forecaster Wang Ping-hsiang told AFP.
"The greatest impact is expected in Taipei, New Taipei, Keelung and Yilan, while the heaviest rainfall is forecast for mountainous areas in central and northern Taiwan."
Bavi's strong-wind radius of 380 kilometres will make it the largest typhoon to hit Taiwan in more than 30 years.
Many schools and businesses were shuttered on Friday across northern and eastern Taiwan, and hundreds of flights were cancelled.
In Keelung, locals flocked to a fresh food market to buy fruit and vegetables, street vendors secured their stands, and a temple covered and tied down an outdoor statue ahead of the storm.
"What you're seeing now is the most remarkable sight we haven't seen in 10 years," Keelung restaurant owner Penny Pan, 48, said as her husband placed sandbags at the entrance to their eatery.
"In the past we never used sandbags to prepare for typhoons. But this time they're forecasting Force 10 gusts, so the captains and fishermen have all been saying we need to be better prepared," Pan said.
- 'Strong winds and heavy rain' -
Bavi is expected to dump up to nearly a metre of rain, raising concerns about potential flooding and landslides.
People were ordered to stay away from the shore as the typhoon whipped up to nine metre (30 foot) waves.
More than 2,000 people have been evacuated from their homes, mostly in the mountainous county of Hualien in Taiwan's east, where authorities are monitoring two barrier dams.
Taiwanese President Lai Ching-te urged people in areas likely to be most affected by Bavi to remain on "high alert."
More than 28,000 troops, along with machinery, equipment and vehicles, were on standby to respond to emergencies.
"We're worried," said Samuel Fu, who works in a noodle shop in the coastal district of Bali, in New Taipei City.
"This is the first time we've experienced such a big typhoon... since we opened the shop," 20-year-old Fu told AFP.
On Japan's remote Sakashima islands, schools and offices were closed in some areas as the typhoon approached, with forecasters warning of high waves, storms and landslides.
"We've covered objects with nets so that they won't fly away...the storm is getting gradually stronger right now," Masaru Nakamura, who works at a hotel on Miyako island, told AFP.
On Okinawa, Koki Ohama said his water sports and barbeque business in the major city of Naha would suffer from the storm.
"We were originally fully reserved this weekend, but bookings were all cancelled, so it's painful," the 30-year-old said.
After slamming into the Japanese islands and sweeping past Taiwan's northern tip, Bavi is expected to make landfall in eastern China over the weekend.
Extreme weather has already wreaked havoc on southern and central China this week, with storms leaving at least 39 dead and causing dozens of rivers to overflow and a reservoir dam to burst.
Oceans experienced their hottest June on record and could set fresh highs in the months ahead, the European Union's Copernicus Marine Service said last week.
Warmer oceans help tropical storms to intensify and add more moisture, which can fall as heavy rain.
Adding to the mix is the return this year of El Nino, a natural climate phenomenon that warms Pacific surface temperatures and typically occurs every two to seven years.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor polls: 2.7 mil voters ready to decide state's future
Article:
JOHOR BARU: More than 2.7 million registered voters, including a significant number of first-time voters, will determine Johor's future as preparations for the 16th Johor election enter the final stage.
The Election Commission (EC) has intensified its final checks on ballot boxes and election equipment ahead of polling day, with EC chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun leading inspection visits at several locations in Pontian and Kukup.
Accompanied by EC members and senior management, Ramlan inspected ballot boxes at Dewan Jubli Intan in Pontian and Dewan Serbaguna Permas in Kukup.
EC deputy chairman Dr Azmi Sharom also carried out similar inspections at SMK Bandar Dato' Onn and the Majlis Bandaraya Iskandar Puteri (MBIP).
The inspections are part of the EC's final preparations to ensure all election materials, including ballot boxes, are ready for use across Johor's 56 state constituencies.
The Johor election will see a diverse electorate, with young voters expected to play a crucial role in determining the outcome.
Based on the latest electoral roll, Johor has about 2.73 million registered voters, including approximately 544,657 aged between 21 and 29, representing nearly 20 per cent of the electorate.
A large proportion of this group comprises young voters who gained voting rights under the Undi18 policy, which automatically lowered the voting age from 21 to 18.
The younger electorate is expected to be closely watched by political parties, as many are first-time voters with no previous voting history and may be less tied to traditional party loyalties.
Political observers have said young voters are likely to be influenced by local issues, candidate visibility, employment opportunities, cost of living pressures and social media narratives.
The EC has set polling day for tomorrow (July 11), with early voting having been completed on July 7.
The 16th Johor election will see 172 candidates contesting 56 state seats, with voters deciding the composition of the next Johor government.
With campaigning ending at 11.59pm tonight, all eyes will now turn to voter turnout as parties make their final push to mobilise supporters ahead of polling day.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar makes final appeal for Johoreans to choose capable leadership
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has urged Johoreans to make a decisive choice at the state election tomorrow, saying their votes will determine the next chapter of the state's future.
Anwar, who is also Pakatan Harapan (PH) chairman, called on Johoreans, including those living outside the state, to return home and exercise their right to vote for leaders who are trustworthy, capable and committed to serving the people.
"Tomorrow is a historic day that will determine the next chapter in the story of Johor's future and its people," he said in a final appeal ahead of polling day.
He said PH had proven its ability to govern at the federal level through the Madani administration by implementing reforms, attracting investments, raising wages, creating more dignified employment opportunities and resolving long-delayed projects.
Anwar said the government had also succeeded in restoring Malaysia's international standing and confidence among global investors.
"The spirit of reform and good governance is what we will bring to elevate Johor to a higher level," he said.
The PH chairman urged voters to give the coalition the mandate to lead Johor with integrity, transparency and fairness, while ensuring equal opportunities for all communities.
He also called on voters to reject leaders who defended those accused of misusing public funds and parties that relied on racial and religious sentiments to gain support.
"This election is an opportunity to reject those who protect thieves of the people's money, and reject politics that is bankrupt of ideas and depends only on stirring hatred for votes," he said.
Anwar ended his message with a call for Johoreans to choose a leadership that could deliver a more prosperous, fair and united future for the state.
"May Allah bless Johor and grant this state an honest leadership that will open a new chapter of prosperity, justice and wellbeing for all Bangsa Johor," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ringgit ends firmer as safe haven demand for US dollar fades
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit ended the week on a stronger note against the US dollar on Friday, as safe-haven demand for the greenback faded.
At 6pm, the local currency climbed to 4.0695/0745 against the US dollar from 4.0750/0795 at Thursday's close.
AmBank Group FX strategist Yim Soon Kah told Bernama that the weaker US dollar was driven by reports that US and Iranian mediators would continue peace negotiations despite the recent military escalation, reducing demand for the greenback as a safe-haven currency and for higher-yielding US Treasuries.
On the domestic front, he noted that the market reaction was generally neutral following yesterday's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) meeting, with Bank Negara Malaysia keeping the overnight policy rate unchanged, in line with market expectations.
SPI Asset Management managing partner Stephen Innes said the ringgit had reversed some of its recent risk-off weakness after the United States launched extensive air strikes in response to Iran's missile attacks on three energy tankers.
He said the local currency's rebound had been supported by easing oil prices, suggesting that markets were not yet pricing in a prolonged escalation in West Asia. "As geopolitical concerns have moderated, some of the safe-haven demand for the US dollar has faded," Innes told Bernama.
At the close, the ringgit traded mostly higher against a basket of major currencies.
It was lower against the Japanese yen at 2.5148/5181 from 2.5091/5120 at Thursday's close, but appreciated against the British pound to 5.4572/4639 from 5.4654/4714, and strengthened against the euro to 4.6502/6559 from 4.6581/6633.
The ringgit traded mostly lower against regional currencies.
It marginally dropped against the Singapore dollar to 3.1520/1561 from 3.1518/1558 at Thursday's close and depreciated against the Indonesian rupiah to 225.2/225.6 from 224.8/225.1.
It slipped against the Thai baht to 12.2101/2302 from 12.1886/2064 and was unchanged against the Philippine peso at 6.61/6.62.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: OPEC+ countries produced oil 7.5mil bpd below target in June – IEA
Article:
MOSCOW: OPEC+ countries with voluntary restrictions increased oil production in June by 2.1 million barrels per day to 32.4 million barrels, and produced 7.5 million barrels per day below the quota level, the International Energy Agency (IEA) said in its fresh report on Friday, Sputnik/RIA Novosti reported.
According to report, the total OPEC+ oil production in June amounted to 32.44 million barrels per day against 30.3 million barrels in May.
The oil production of the 17 parties to the agreement (excluding Mexico, Iran, Libya and Venezuela) increased during this period to 26.38 million barrels per day from 24.21 million barrels per day.
However, the target quota level, taking into account the voluntary restrictions of the seven countries and compensation schedules for them, was 33.89 million barrels in June. Thus, OPEC+ countries produced 7.51 million barrels less than the target.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MARKET PULSE PM JULY 10, 2026 [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia closed higher, buoyed by a strong rebound in banking stocks that lifted the broader market, in line with gains across regional bourses and Wall Street.
Investor sentiment improved as concerns over escalating geopolitical tensions eased, with oil prices stabilising toward the end of a volatile week.
The ringgit closed firmer against the US dollar at 4.0660.
In the crypto market, Bitcoin rebounded to RM260,765.
Ethereum climbed to RM7,257, while Solana was up at RM322.
That wraps up today's Market Pulse.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Man nearly sucked out of 'detached' window on Ryanair flight
Article:
THESSALONIKI, Greece: A man was nearly sucked out the window of a Ryanair flight when it "detached" mid-air en route to Germany, with other passengers pulling him back inside, witnesses and officials said Friday.
The passenger, described as a tourist from Serbia on a flight from Thessaloniki in Greece to Memmingen in Germany, has been hospitalised with friction burns but was otherwise in good condition, authorities said.
"Most of us had fallen asleep, we had closed our eyes. There was a noise, like a tyre bursting," a fellow passenger told Radio Thessaloniki.
"We immediately realised there had been a decompression. There were screams ... for a moment I thought someone had accidentally opened the emergency door," the woman said.
"The masks dropped and there was a strong smell. The head and shoulders of one passenger were outside the window. Fortunately, he hadn't taken off his seat belt."
Other passengers near the man helped to pull him in, she said.
Greek media reported the incident had occurred over North Macedonia, and said the window had been broken by a piece of debris that detached from one of the plane's engines.
Ryanair in a statement said the flight "returned to Thessaloniki shortly after takeoff when a passenger window detached during the flight. The aircraft landed normally and the passengers returned to the terminal."
A replacement aircraft was made available to transport the remaining passengers to Memmingen, the Irish carrier said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Advancecon lands RM121.7mil Port Dickson job
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Advancecon Holdings Bhd has bagged a RM121.66 million subcontract to undertake earthworks and associated works for an off-river storage (ORS) facility supporting a data centre water supply development in Port Dickson, Negri Sembilan.
In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, the company said its unit Advancecon Infra Sdn Bhd had accepted a letter of award from the main contractor.
The subcontract covers the construction and completion of earthworks and related works for the ORS under the proposed water supply scheme development with a production capacity of 65 million litres per day for a data centre at Linggi, Port Dickson.
The works are scheduled for completion within 30 months from the date of written instruction for physical works and mobilisation.
Advancecon said the customer has more than 30 years of experience in the construction industry and has undertaken major infrastructure projects in Malaysia.
This includes the PKNS Cyber Valley development, the raw water pipeline contract for the Rasau water treatment plant and finishing works for the MRT Line 2 project in Cyberjaya.
Advancecon said the subcontract is expected to contribute positively to its earnings over the contract period, barring any unforeseen circumstances.
The project will be funded through internally generated funds and/or external borrowings.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: US-Iran escalation could threaten 2027 oil market surplus – IEA
Article:
LONDON: An escalation of hostilities between the US and Iran could upend the International Energy Agency's forecast of a significant oil market surplus next year, it said on Friday, as global supply jumped in June when the Strait of Hormuz reopened but still lagged pre-war levels.
Global oil markets received some respite last month as a peace agreement between the US and Iran facilitated the reopening of the strait, the effective closure of which had cut as much as 14 million barrels per day (bpd) of crude flows during the peak of the largest oil supply crisis in history.
The IEA said global oil supply rose by 4.1 million bpd in June, but remained 9.4 million bpd below pre-war levels.
LASTING PEACE DEAL NEEDED FOR NORMALISATION
The agency predicts supply will expand by 7.5 million bpd next year after a 3.7 million bpd contraction this year, but that is contingent on improved Hormuz transits.
"An escalation in hostilities on July 7-8, however, clouds the outlook and could upend the forecast that sees the market flipping to a surplus next year," it said, adding that a lasting peace agreement is a "must" for oil markets to normalise.
The IEA's 2027 forecasts imply that supply will outweigh demand by 4.62 million bpd next year from an 860,000 bpd deficit this year, provided producers can restart fields and refiners can resume normal product shipments.
The Paris-based agency, which advises industrialised nations, sees global oil demand falling by one million bpd this year, before rebounding to rise two million bpd in 2027.
In the nearer term, it sees the peak summer fuel demand season, together with lower prices, lifting consumption by around eight million bpd compared with May's low point at the peak of the crisis.
"Much lower oil prices are also incentivising growth in oil use, as is a brightening economic outlook," it said.
Brent crude futures were trading slightly lower on the day on Friday at US$75.85 by 0934 GMT. Rival forecaster OPEC will release its own monthly oil market report on July 13.
REFINING CRUNCH BUOYS FUEL MARGINS
Refining activity and oil product shipments were slower to react to the reopening of Hormuz than crude oil exports, the IEA said, which, combined with the peak summer demand season, has tightened the market for refined fuels and pushed up refining profit margins.
"The disconnect between apparently well-supplied crude oil markets and tight product markets underpinned a rally in cracks and refinery margins to four-year highs by early July," it said.
Concerns over jet fuel shortages have been replaced by worries about tightening supplies of petrol and diesel, the IEA added.
Diesel markets in the Atlantic basin have tightened rapidly in recent weeks due to restricted Middle East output being compounded by a collapse in Russian exports, as Ukraine intensified its attacks on Russia's refining infrastructure.
Meanwhile, the flood of crude oil sailing to destinations after Hormuz reopened saw global inventories rise for the first time in four months in June, the IEA said, by 21 million barrels. That comes after a cumulative 360-million-barrel draw in stocks over the March-May period.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: SoftBank, PayPay in talks to invest in Seven & i
Article:
TOKYO: SoftBank Corp and mobile payments operator PayPay are in talks to invest in retail giant Seven & i Holdings, Bloomberg News reported on Friday.
The investment will likely total several hundred billion yen and Sumitomo Mitsui Card, a unit of Sumitomo Mitsui Financial Group, may also take a stake, Bloomberg said.
Reuters could not immediately verify the report. SoftBank and SMFG declined to comment, while Seven & i did not respond to a request for comment.
Seven & i operates the 7-Eleven convenience store chain in countries worldwide, with Japan and the US its largest markets.
SoftBank is aiming to use its in-house artificial intelligence to improve store management and introduce autonomous robots to reduce manpower at Seven & i stores, Bloomberg said.
SoftBank Corp, the domestic telecommunications arm of SoftBank Group, has been developing AI tools for enterprise clients with ChatGPT-maker OpenAI.
OpenAI has been the focus of a major investment push, with SoftBank Group's cumulative investment commitment set to exceed US$60 billion by the end of 2026.
The potential investment comes a year after the end of Seven & i's prolonged tussle with Canadian convenience store rival Alimentation Couche-Tard, which had sought to acquire it in what would have been Japan's largest-ever foreign buyout.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bursa Malaysia closes higher on rebound in financial stocks
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia closed higher on Friday, supported by a strong rebound in financial services stocks that lifted the overall market sentiment, in line with gains across regional markets.
At 5pm, the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) rose 13.85 points, or 0.83 per cent, to 1,691.49  from Thursday's close of 1,677.64.
The benchmark index opened 5.14 points better at 1,682.78, marking its intraday low, and hit its highest level of 1,696.25 during the midday trading session.
Market breadth was positive, with gainers beating losers 648 to 443, while 581 counters were unchanged, 1,044 untraded, and 25 suspended.
Turnover increased to 2.85 billion units valued at RM2.25 billion from 2.64 billion units valued at RM2.19 billion on Wednesday.
IPPFA Sdn Bhd director of investment strategy and country economist Mohd Sedek Jantan said Bank Negara Malaysia's decision to maintain the overnight policy rate (OPR) at 2.75 per cent yesterday provided greater policy certainty for investors and businesses amid concerns over global energy prices and operating costs.
"Adding to the positive tone, foreign investors turned net buyers for a second consecutive trading session after an extended period of persistent net selling.
"This represents an early sign of improving confidence in Malaysian equities, supported by the country's resilient macroeconomic fundamentals, stable monetary policy and relatively attractive market valuations," he told Bernama.
Mohd Sedek also said that market sentiment improved as concerns over escalating geopolitical tensions eased after oil prices stabilised towards the end of a volatile week.
"Brent crude traded near US$76 per barrel after falling more than two per cent in the previous session, as the United States and Iran continued technical negotiations despite renewed fighting that had briefly disrupted shipping activity through the Strait of Hormuz," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Fire dept readies 667 assets to take on Super El Nino phenomenon
Article:
SHAH ALAM: The Fire and Rescue Department has deployed 667 assets nationwide in preparation for the potential impact of the Super El Nino phenomenon, which is expected to affect Malaysia from early next year.
Its director-general, Datuk Seri Nor Hisham Mohammad, said all state directors have been instructed to strategically position assets in high-risk areas, particularly in the northern states of Peninsular Malaysia and Sabah's east coast.
"The department has made the necessary preparations, and I have personally met with zone chiefs across the country to review their early preparations before we begin facing the impact of Super El Nino from January to April next year.
"So far, there has been no impact because Super El Nino will occur in the Pacific Ocean. If forecasts for November and December are accurate, its effects will reach Malaysia in January, when northern areas such as Perlis and Kedah typically become dry, causing padi straw and scrubland to become highly susceptible to fires," he said.
Nor Hisham was speaking to reporters after the 2026 Governance, Integrity and Anti-Corruption Dialogue programme at the Dahlia Auditorium, Sultan Salahuddin Abdul Aziz Shah Mosque, here.
Northern states such as Perlis and Kedah are expected to experience drier conditions, increasing the risk of paddy straw and bush fires, while Sabah's east coast is forecast to see a significant decline in rainfall between March and April next year, he added.
To prepare for the situation, he said the department plans to deploy water tanker vehicles to 183 critical locations, optimise the use of more than 600 portable pumps, and strengthen its aerial logistics with 10 helicopters, which are expected to be operational by December.
He added that they were also strengthening cooperation with the Drainage and Department (DID), the armed forces and the Forestry Department to accelerate firefighting operations and enhance enforcement against open burning.
"If there are water supply disruptions or low water pressure, the Fire Department Control Centre will supply water to critical areas and deploy fire engines from other zones in advance without waiting for the situation to worsen," he said.
On another matter, Nor Hisham said the department recorded a 25 per cent reduction in disciplinary cases during the first six months of this year, attributed to the implementation of integrity awareness programmes and continuous inspections at 336 fire stations nationwide.
He said, as of June this year, only 15 disciplinary cases had been brought before the Disciplinary Board, compared with 36 cases for the whole of 2025, 43 cases in 2024, and 41 cases in 2023.
He added that complaints received fell into eight categories — absenteeism, drug abuse, insubordination, violent crimes, bankruptcy, dishonesty, syariah-related offences, and "mule account" cases, which are currently being investigated in collaboration with the police.
"Of the 15 cases, seven personnel received warnings, three had their salary increments deferred, three were dismissed from service, and two had their emoluments forfeited. Every case is carefully reviewed to ensure disciplinary action is taken fairly and based on strong evidence," he said.
Nor Hisham added that the decline was also driven by intervention measures through special teams deployed to selected fire stations to assess management practices, workforce organisation, and financial administration, thereby reducing the trend of complaints among department personnel.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor election: Johoreans in Singapore return home to cast ballots
Article:
JOHOR BARU: A day before polling day for the Johor election, Johoreans working in Singapore have begun making their way home to cast their ballots.
Many are willing to endure long journeys to fulfil their responsibility as voters.
Checks at Larkin Sentral and the Sultan Iskandar Building Customs, Immigration and Quarantine Complex found that passenger and vehicle movements had increased since this morning.
A Larkin Sentral management spokesman said the number of passengers had increased by about five to 10 per cent compared with normal days, driven by the return of Johoreans from Singapore.
Most voters interviewed said they had taken leave and planned their journeys earlier to avoid congestion while ensuring they could fulfil their responsibilities as voters.
Nur Arissa Hanani, 25, said returning from Singapore was not merely about casting her ballot but also about determining Johor's future.
"As a Johorean, it is only right for me to return home to vote because this is our opportunity to choose capable leaders for Johor's future.
"Although I have to travel back and forth from Singapore, I see it as my responsibility as a Johorean," she said.
She said demand for bus tickets had also increased as many people took the opportunity to return home earlier.
"I noticed yesterday that bus tickets were already fully booked. Many took the opportunity to return earlier because they realised how important voting is," she said.
Mohd Amzar Zahayudin, 22, said the journey back from Singapore was congested, especially during peak hours, but this did not deter him from returning to vote.
"I hope whoever is chosen later can carry out their responsibilities well and continue working in the people's interests so that Johor can continue to progress," he said.
The Malaysian Highway Authority expects more than 300,000 outstation voters to return to fulfil their responsibilities, which is expected to contribute to increased traffic flow on highways.
Tomorrow, some 2.7 million eligible voters are expected to cast their ballots at 1,076 polling centres across the state for the 16th Johor election.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Perkeso's Lindung 24 covers 2.27mil workers, collects RM58mil
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Social Security Organisation (Perkeso) has collected nearly RM58 million contributions under the Non-Employment Injury scheme (Lindung 24 Jam) since its implementation, with more than 2.27 million workers now covered under it.
Perkeso group chief executive Datuk Seri Dr Mohammed Azman Aziz Mohammed said as of July 9, a total of 207,000 employers had registered for the scheme, covering 1.92 million Malaysian workers and 307,000 foreign workers.
"We have received 1,056 claims under the scheme, with some applications still undergoing assessment and approval," he said at a press conference here, today.
The update comes after the government decided to make participation in Lindung 24 optional for Malaysian workers while maintaining mandatory coverage for foreign workers.
Responding to concerns over delays in claims, Mohammed Azman said Perkeso actively assists claimants instead of waiting for them to come forward.
He urged members of the public to report directly to him if they encountered delays or unnecessary bureaucracy in the claims process.
"I do not understand who says it is difficult to make claims. Where exactly is the difficulty? As I have said before, we do not wait for contributors to come to us. We go to the ground to help them.
"I have made this clear on my social media platforms, including TikTok. If anyone experiences delays or unnecessary bureaucracy despite being eligible, report it directly to me.
"I will not compromise with officers who delay claims or create unnecessary bureaucracy," he said.
Mohammed Azman added that while Perkeso is committed to expediting claims, the agency must still comply with the Employees' Social Security Act 1969 and establish procedures when processing applications.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia-Thai prawn, sea bass issues to be resolved through science
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Malaysia and Thailand are in talks to address concerns surrounding the trade of sea bass and prawns, with both countries working towards an amicable resolution.
Fisheries director-general Datuk Adnan Hussain said the discussions focused on enhancing technical requirements to facilitate trade without compromising biosecurity, food safety and aquatic animal health.
"Malaysia views the process as a technical coordination effort between the competent authorities of both countries and not as a trade dispute.
"The approach taken is based on science, risk management and shared interests to ensure the fisheries trade relationship continues to grow in a fair, transparent and mutually beneficial manner," he said in a statement today.
Adnan said Malaysia had submitted 35 technical questions concerning the prawn trade to seek further clarification following an initial assessment of the information provided by Thailand, before finalising any risk assessment.
As for the sea bass trade, he said the two neighbouring countries were refining several import requirements, including those related to certification, food safety and risk management.
Adnan said Malaysia had also put forward several proposals to strengthen technical cooperation, including harmonising certification requirements, enhancing traceability systems, sharing scientific data and adopting a more risk-based inspection mechanism.
"Malaysia hopes this approach will pave the way for a review of the 100 per cent sampling requirement for sea bass exporters with good compliance records, while exploring mechanisms such as green lanes for farms that meet food safety and biosecurity standards," he said.
Adnan stressed that all decisions taken by Malaysia would be based on scientific risk assessments and international standards.
"Once all the necessary technical information is received, the assessment process will be expedited to facilitate smoother trade and safeguard the interests of the country's aquaculture industry," he said.
Adnan  said that through the negotiations, Malaysia also hoped to resolve the issue of Thailand's ban on Malaysian prawns, which has been in effect since 2017, through a common technical approach that would create more balanced market access for the aquaculture industries of both countries.
"The government, through the Department of Fisheries Malaysia (DOF), will continue to work with relevant ministries and agencies to strengthen export market access while assisting operators in improving compliance with export standards, traceability systems and biosecurity practices to meet international market requirements," he said.
Yesterday, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim and his Thai counterpart Anutin Charnvirakul agreed to expedite the resolution of outstanding fisheries market access issues during a meeting held in conjunction with Anutin's official visit to Malaysia.
On May 16, Malaysia tightened import controls on Thai fisheries products by requiring Certificates of Analysis (CoA) for sea bass and imposing a temporary import restriction on five specific shrimp species.
The temporary restriction covers Penaeus esculentes (brown tiger prawn), Fenneropenaeus merguiensis (banana prawn), Penaeus vannamei (whiteleg shrimp), Penaeus monodon (giant tiger prawn) and Penaeus stylirostris (blue shrimp).
Thailand is among the key markets for Malaysian sea bass exports, with bilateral trade reaching RM92.6 million involving 9,286 tonnes in 2024, while trade value recorded up to 2025 stood at RM42.6 million involving 4,320 tonnes.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia Airlines cancels Kuala Lumpur-Taipei flights due to Typhoon Bavi
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia Airlines has cancelled its flights between Kuala Lumpur and Taipei scheduled for July 11 due to adverse weather conditions associated with Typhoon Bavi.
In a travel advisory issued today, the airline said the affected flights were MH366 (Kuala Lumpur-Taipei) and MH367 (Taipei-Kuala Lumpur).
"Given the evolving weather situation, the airline is closely monitoring developments. Further updates regarding flight operations will be communicated directly to affected passengers," it said.
Malaysia Airlines advised passengers to update their contact details via the 'My Booking' section on its website or mobile application to receive timely updates via email and SMS.
"Affected passengers may opt for a refund or rebook their travel on the same route at no additional cost within 30 days from the original travel date through 'My Booking', AskMH Live Chat or the airline's Global Contact Centre," it noted.
The national carrier reiterated that the safety of its passengers and crew remains its highest priority.
Bavi is forecast to move northwest at a speed of 15-20 km per hour and make landfall along the coast from Xiapu in Fujian to Wenling in Zhejiang on Saturday night, with intensity weakening after landfall.
From Friday afternoon to Saturday afternoon, gales are expected in waters east of Taiwan, most parts of the East China Sea and waters near Diaoyu Dao, as well as along the coasts of Taiwan, Zhejiang and Fujian.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: High Court upholds 12-year jail term for religious healer who raped teen
Article:
SHAH ALAM: A 58-year-old religious healer will remain behind bars for 12 years after the High Court dismissed his appeal against his conviction and sentence for raping a 17-year-old girl four years ago.
Judge Dr Hazlina Hussain ruled that there was no basis to interfere with the Sessions Court's decision, which found the appellant guilty of sexually assaulting the girl during what was purportedly a healing session to rid her of a spirit.
Hazlina said the victim was a credible witness and the prosecution had successfully proved its case beyond reasonable doubt.
"This court is of the view that there was no misdirection on the part of the Sessions Court trial judge. There is no justification to disagree with the findings of fact as the trial judge had the benefit of seeing and hearing the witnesses testify firsthand and was able to observe their demeanour.
"When the victim testified in court, approximately two years and six months after the incident, she was already 19 years old and employed.
"It is therefore clear that her level of maturity and emotional development also played a role," said the judge in her grounds of judgment on the judiciary website.
Hazlina said the victim's testimony was not assessed in isolation but was evaluated together with other corroborative evidence, including the police report, the victim's conduct following the incident and the medical report, which confirmed the presence of vaginal tears.
The appellant was appealing against the entirety of the Sessions Court's decision, arguing that there was no penetration.
He was ordered to serve the jail term from his arrest on July 29 last year, three years after a police report was lodged against him.
He was exempted from caning because he is over 50 years old.
The offence framed under Section 376(1) of the Penal Code provides for a jail term of not less than five years and not more than 30 years, as well as whipping, upon conviction.
According to the facts of the case, about 10.30pm on April 21, 2022, the appellant went to the victim's house to purportedly perform a healing session after being introduced to her father by another religious healer.
He asked the victim's father to leave the room before sexually assaulting the girl. He remained in the room until about 4am.
The victim did not disclose the incident until about two months later, when her father suggested taking her to Port Dickson for a second healing session.
She then revealed what had happened, and her father lodged a police report on June 16, 2022.
The prosecution was represented by deputy public prosecutor Tengku Najiha Tengku Zahari, while lawyer Khairul Amin Abdullah acted for the appellant.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor election: Bersama unlikely to win seats but could split votes
Article:
KLUANG: Parti Bersama Malaysia (Bersama) may not emerge as winners in the Johor election, but the party could upset the calculations of major coalitions by splitting votes in key battleground constituencies, political analysts said.
While the party is unlikely to secure any seats in its maiden state election outing, analysts believe its presence could prove significant in marginal constituencies where victory margins are narrow and a small shift in support could determine the outcome.
They said the key question was not whether Bersama could form the next Johor government, but which political coalition would be most affected by its participation.
Universiti Teknologi Malaysia Faculty of Social Sciences and Humanities associate professor Dr Mazlan Ali said Bersama's greatest impact would likely be felt in marginal seats where winning margins were below 1,000 votes.
"Bersama is unlikely to win any of the 15 seats it is contesting, but it can still influence the outcome by drawing a portion of votes away from Pakatan Harapan.
"In constituencies where PH previously won by fewer than 1,000 votes, even a small swing could become significant," he told the New Straits Times.
Mazlan said Bersama remained a relatively new party with limited grassroots machinery, relying largely on online campaigning and social media engagement.
"The party appeals more to urban and semi-urban voters who are active online.
"It does not yet have the grassroots strength to penetrate Felda settlements, rural Malay villages, Indian estates or traditional Chinese communities."
He said Bersama's campaign themes of institutional reform, transparency and anti-corruption closely resembled those promoted by PH, making it more likely to compete for the same pool of voters.
"Unlike Perikatan Nasional and Barisan Nasional , which frequently campaign on Malay-Muslim narratives, Bersama positions itself around governance reforms, integrity and institutional change.
"Those are issues that traditionally resonate with PH supporters."
However, Mazlan said the extent of Bersama's impact would vary depending on the constituency.
"In larger urban seats with sizeable electorates, taking several thousand votes may not significantly alter the result if PH enjoys a comfortable majority.
"But in marginal constituencies, Bersama's presence could prove decisive by splitting the opposition vote."
He said it remained difficult to determine exactly which demographic groups Bersama would attract during its electoral debut.
"The key question is whether Bersama will take votes from PKR supporters, urban middle-class Malays, Chinese voters who previously backed PH, or even disillusioned BN supporters.
"At this stage, it is difficult to quantify because the party has never been tested in a state election of this scale."
Senior lecturer at Universiti Malaya's Department of Political Science, Public Administration and Development Studies, Dr Mohammad Tawfik Yaakub also said PH was likely to be more affected by Bersama's entry than its rivals.
He said the party, led by former senior PKR figures, was better positioned to attract reform-minded voters, fence-sitters, professionals and younger Malaysians who had previously supported PH.
"Relatively speaking, I believe PH will feel the greater impact because Bersama is led by former PKR leaders and is more likely to attract reformist voters, middle-class professionals, fence-sitters and younger voters who previously supported PH.
"BN still enjoys the advantage of having an established party machinery and a loyal traditional support base, while PN is likely to be affected only in a limited number of constituencies," he said.
However, Tawfik said the actual impact would depend on Bersama's ability to convince voters that it offered a genuinely distinct alternative from existing political coalitions.
He also agreed that under Malaysia's first-past-the-post electoral system, Bersama could still shape election outcomes without winning seats.
"Even if Bersama fails to secure any seats, it could still influence the outcome by splitting votes, particularly in three-cornered or multi-cornered contests.
"If Bersama manages to secure even a small percentage of votes from PH's traditional support base, it could hand BN an advantage in marginal constituencies.
"On the other hand, if it succeeds in attracting voters who would otherwise have abstained or remained undecided, the impact on the major coalitions may be less pronounced.
"So while Bersama may not emerge as the decisive winner in many constituencies, its presence could still influence victory margins and ultimately determine the outcome in several closely contested seats."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: RM6.19mil frozen pork seized in Miri warehouse raid
Article:
MIRI: Police arrested a local man and seized RM6.19 million worth of frozen pork products suspected of being imported without valid permits during an operation at a warehouse here last night.
The 26-year-old suspect was detained at 8pm during the operation, codenamed Op Taring Chiller.
Internal Security and Public Order Department director Datuk Seri Mohd Yusri Hassan Basri said the operation reflects the police's commitment to strengthening enforcement against illegal food products and the storage of frozen food of unknown halal status.
Inspections at the premises uncovered 30,000kg of cooked frozen pork large intestines, 18,000kg of pork loin packed in 15 pallets, 750kg of pork in one pallet, and several pallets of bone-in pork bellies and frozen pork shoulder.
"The raid was carried out by the Wildlife Crime Bureau/Special Investigation Intelligence, with assistance from the Miri district police headquarters and the Sarawak Veterinary Services Department's Miri Division," Yusri said in a statement today.
He added that the case is being investigated under Sections 9(1) and 9(3) of the Veterinary Public Health Ordinance 1999.
A RM25,000 compound notice was also issued under the same ordinance.
Meanwhile, Yusri said the bureau conducted 201 raids nationwide between Jan 1 and July 10, resulting in 375 arrests.
The operations involved various offences, including the smuggling of non-duty-paid goods, leakage of controlled and subsidised items, frozen food violations, wildlife smuggling, and e-waste, with total seizures estimated at RM264.86 million.
"The police will continue to intensify intelligence gathering, monitoring, and integrated enforcement operations to combat smuggling activities and organised crime more effectively," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Fahmi heads Penang honours list, late Nordin Ahmad receives Datukship
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil heads the 2026 Penang state honours list as the sole recipient of the Darjah Panglima Pangkuan Negeri (DPPN).
State Legislative Assembly Speaker Datuk Seri Law Choo Kiang said the Yang di-Pertua Negeri Tun Ramli Ngah Talib had consented to the 2026 Penang State Honours, Stars and Medals Investiture Ceremony being held over four days on July 11, 13, 14 and 15 in conjunction with the Governor's 85th birthday celebration.
He said a total of 1,221 individuals would receive state honours, stars and medals, comprising 101 recipients of state orders and another 1,120 recipients of stars and medals.
"The recipients comprise members of the state and federal civil service, politicians, corporate figures, non-governmental organisation members, local entrepreneurs and volunteers," he said in a statement today.
Law said Fahmi was the sole recipient of the DPPN, which carries the title Datuk Seri.
He said 19 recipients would receive the Darjah Gemilang Pangkuan Negeri (DGPN), which also carries the title Datuk Seri.
Among them are Mara Incorporated Sdn Bhd chairman Datuk Wira Abdul Halim Mohammad, InvestPenang chief executive officer Datuk Loo Lee Lian, Pelita Samudra Pertama (M) Sdn Bhd managing director Tan Sri Datuk Murugan Doraisamy, Malaya Chief Judge Datuk Seri Hashim Hamzah, Menara Rezeki Sdn Bhd managing director Datuk Saiful Nizam Mohd Yusoff and M Summit Group chairman Datuk Moh See Eng.
Another nine recipients will receive the Darjah Yang Mulia Pangkuan Negeri (DMPN), which carries the title Datuk.
Among them are former Penang police chief Datuk Azizee Ismail and UEM Group Bhd managing director Datuk Amran Hafiz Affifudin.
Law said 72 recipients would be conferred the Darjah Setia Pangkuan Negeri (DSPN), which also carries the title Datuk.
Among the DSPN recipients are the late veteran Malay film actor, director and scriptwriter Nordin Ahmad, Penang deputy state secretary (Development) Mohamed Abdul Rahman, Seberang Prai City Council secretary Mohd Ibrahim Md Nor, National Disaster Management Agency (Nadma) director-general Meor Ismail Meor Akim, Penang Development Corporation chief executive officer Abdul Latiff Abd Aziz, SRS LRT Sdn Bhd project director Adil Putra Ahmad; Penang Skills Development Centre chief executive officer Dr Hari Narayanan P. Ondiveeran, IJM Land Bhd chief executive officer Datuk Ling Thou Lung, Bank Rakyat chairman Datuk Mohd Irwan Mohd Mubarak, Ancom Nylex Bhd managing director and chief executive officer Datuk Lee Cheun Wei, Vygon global director Thanapathy Kumaraiah and Silcotech Industry Sdn Bhd executive director Yeoh Lik Jin.
Law said 92 recipients would receive the Darjah Johan Negeri (DJN), followed by 31 recipients of the Bintang Cemerlang Negeri (BCN).
He said 194 people would receive the Pingat Kelakuan Terpuji (PKT), 328 recipients of the Pingat Jasa Kebaktian (PJK), 463 recipients of the Pingat Jasa Masyarakat (PJM) and 12 recipients of the Pingat Bakti Setia (PBS).
"The full list of recipients is contained in the official souvenir book for the 2026 Penang State Honours, Stars and Medals Investiture Ceremony," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: EAIC finds procedural lapses in footballers' citizenship applications
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Enforcement Agency Integrity Commission (EAIC) has found procedural irregularities in the citizenship applications of seven naturalised footballers, including an unusually fast assessment process and lapses in immigration and citizenship requirements.
In a statement today, the EAIC said its special task force, which investigated the National Registration Department's (NRD) compliance with standard operating procedures (SOPs), found the applications were processed within a very short timeframe.
"Among the irregularities identified were those involving the entry permit issuance process, including interview and security screening procedures conducted by the Immigration Department, as well as the department's security screening process and the Malay Language Knowledge Test administered by the NRD for citizenship approval.
"There was also non-compliance involving confirmation of the renunciation of citizenship by the naturalised players from the authorities of their respective countries of origin, as well as the submission of passports by the players to the Immigration Department," the EAIC said.
The commission said its investigation found that the Federal Constitution empowers the Home Minister to consider citizenship applications by naturalisation under Article 19.
"The minister subsequently exercised this authority and discretion by granting special approval, taking into account the applicants' contributions to football, which could enhance the country's reputation, pursuant to Article 19(2), read together with Section 20(1)(e) of Part III of the Second Schedule to the Federal Constitution.
"This provision grants the minister discretionary authority to determine, on grounds generally or specifically specified by the minister, that a period during which a person resided outside the Federation may be counted as a period of residence in Malaysia."
Following the findings, the EAIC recommended that the NRD and the Home Ministry review the entire process and documentation relating to the granting of citizenship by naturalisation to the seven players under Article 19(2) of the Federal Constitution.
It also recommended that guidelines be established for the exercise of discretionary powers under the law, taking into account the fundamental requirements of the Federal Constitution, which emphasise the period of residence in the country as a basis for granting citizenship and as an important factor when considering approvals under special circumstances.
"The NRD and the Home Ministry should establish specific standard operating procedures concerning the granting of citizenship status, as well as the renunciation of original citizenship by foreign nationals who have obtained Malaysian citizenship," it said.
The EAIC said that as the Federal Constitution does not recognise dual citizenship, the NRD should prescribe a specific period within which renunciation of citizenship in the country of origin must be completed, along with appropriate consequences for failure to provide proof of such renunciation.
It added that the NRD must also establish guidelines concerning the exercise of discretionary powers by the registrar-general, particularly in relation to the issuance of birth certificates under Section 10A of the Births and Deaths Registration Act.
"In exercising such discretionary powers, the registrar-general should take into account that the inclusion of this provision in the Act is intended to provide clear legal authority for the registration of births," adding that such discretion should only be exercised after the registrar-general is fully satisfied that the relevant legal requirements have been met.
It added that the Immigration Department and the NRD, together with the police, must create specific SOPs regarding procedures for conducting strict and comprehensive security screening for the purpose of approving entry permits and citizenship.
"EAIC is of the view that the acquisition of citizenship through naturalisation is a matter involving national interests and security. Therefore, the applicable SOPs must be closely monitored to ensure strict compliance by the Immigration Department and the NRD."
The EAIC said the findings report has been submitted to the Home Ministry, the Immigration Department and the NRD for further action.
The task force was set up under Section 17 of the Enforcement Agency Integrity Commission Act 2009, following an investigation into a complaint against the NRD.
The investigation involved the NRD and the Immigration Department, both under the Home Ministry and responsible for processing permanent residency and naturalisation citizenship applications.
The task force examined documents related to the seven players from the NRD and the Football Association of Malaysia, recorded statements from 15 officers and five members of the public, and reviewed relevant laws, regulations and circulars.
The investigation followed controversy in August last year over the eligibility of seven naturalised players — Imanol Machuca, Facundo Garces, Rodrigo Holgado, Jon Irazabal, Joao Figueiredo, Gabriel Palmero and Hector Hevel — who featured for Malaysia under Peter Cklamovski during the 2027 Asian Cup qualifiers, including the 4-0 victory over Vietnam in June 2025.
They are currently serving 12-month suspensions.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Lawyer vows continuity in Mengkibol if elected
Article:
With pix NSTP/Aizuddin Saad
KLUANG: Pakatan Harapan candidate for Mengkibol, Chu Poh Yee, has pledged to continue the work of former assemblyman Chew Chong Sin if she wins the election.
The lawyer, who is making her electoral debut, said many constituents had approached her during the campaign with issues they hoped she could resolve if elected.
"So far, the campaign has gone very smoothly and I have received a very positive response from residents throughout Mengkibol.
"They are already looking forward to having a new assemblyman, and some have started asking whether I can assist them with various issues if I am elected," the 33-year-old told the New Straits Times.
She admitted that being fielded as a first-time candidate initially came with considerable pressure, particularly as she had limited time to introduce herself to voters.
"I have a very strong team behind me, and after meeting more residents throughout the campaign, I feel much calmer and more confident.
She said her previous experience working alongside Chew and Kluang MP Wong Shu Qi on legal and community welfare matters had prepared her for the role.
Chu also called for a healthier political culture, saying election campaigns should focus on policies and public service instead of personal attacks.
She said she maintained cordial relations with rival candidates despite their political differences.

Title: Anwar surprises nine-year-old Felda boy with bicycle
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today shared the heartwarming story of nine-year-old Muhammad Afif Ikhwan, who travelled through an oil palm plantation with his grandmother in the hope of meeting him.
He said the short encounter with the boy during a programme at Felda Palong Timur in Segamat had made a deep impression on him, prompting him to send someone to visit him and present him with a bicycle.
"What may appear to us as a small dream was, in fact, big enough to move his grandmother, Rosimah Mohammad, to ride a motorcycle through an oil palm plantation to fulfil her grandson's wish.
"His little hand reached through the crowd, and we shook hands before bringing him onto the stage at Felda Palong Timur, Segamat, recently.
"I never expected that such a brief moment would carry such great meaning for Afif," he said in a Facebook post today.
No matter how busy we are managing the affairs of the country and facing various major challenges, we must never lose our compassion in reaching out to others and showing humanity, he said.
The Prime Minister also hoped that the encounter would become a cherished memory that would inspire Muhammad Afif to continue his studies and contribute to his family, community and country in the future.
Yesterday, Bernama reported that Rosimah, 58, from Kampung Pudu, Segamat, rode a motorcycle along oil palm plantation tracks solely to give her grandson the opportunity to see the Prime Minister up close.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Dengue cases up 35pc nationwide, 34 deaths recorded
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Malaysia recorded a 34.8 per cent surge in dengue cases this year, with 42,848 cases reported as of yesterday compared with 31,790 during the same period last year.
Dengue-related deaths also jumped by 78.9 per cent to 34, up from 19 fatalities recorded during the corresponding period in 2025, the Health Ministry said in a statement today.
Epidemiological analysis showed that nearly two-thirds, or 63.5 per cent, of all cases nationwide were concentrated in Selangor, Kuala Lumpur, and Putrajaya.
Selangor recorded 19,313 cases, a 43.2 per cent increase from the same period last year, while Kuala Lumpur and Putrajaya reported 7,909 cases, representing an 83.8 per cent spike.
Higher case numbers were also recorded in Johor, Sabah, Negri Sembilan, Perak, Sarawak, Perlis, Terengganu, and Labuan.
"The Klang Valley remains the country's main dengue hotspot and requires more intensive and coordinated control measures," the ministry said.
"Although several states have recorded fewer cases compared with the same period last year, this should not lead to complacency. All states must continue to strengthen and sustain dengue prevention and control measures, as the situation can change rapidly depending on epidemiological, environmental, and weather conditions."
The ministry said the increase aligns with the cyclical epidemiological pattern of dengue, where endemic countries typically experience major outbreaks every three to five years.
These periodic surges are driven by multiple factors, including changes in population immunity, shifts in dominant dengue virus serotypes, and weather conditions like rainfall, temperature, and humidity that favour Aedes mosquito breeding and survival.
Other contributing factors include rapid urbanisation, population movement, environmental conditions, water-holding containers acting as breeding sites, the coverage of vector control programmes, and community participation.
The ministry said it will continue strengthening prevention and control efforts through a whole-of-nation approach under the Integrated Vector Management framework.
This strategy includes enhanced disease and entomological surveillance, early detection of outbreaks, vector control, risk communication, community empowerment, law enforcement, and continuous monitoring.
The ministry also urged communities, local authorities, government agencies, educational institutions, employers, and the private sector to eliminate Aedes breeding sites, conduct regular clean-ups, and seek early medical attention if symptoms develop.
The ministry added that it will continue to closely monitor the situation and intensify control measures to curb transmission, prevent complications, and protect public health.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: DAP fields Mugunthan for Seremban Jaya state seat
Article:
SEREMBAN: Pakatan Harapan has named S. Mugunthan, a research officer to a Negri Sembilan executive councillor, as its candidate for the Seremban Jaya state seat in the upcoming state election on Aug 1.
DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke announced Mugunthan, 40, will replace four-term incumbent Datuk P. Gunasekaran, who is stepping down after representing the constituency since 2008.
Loke expressed confidence that Mugunthan would retain the seat for DAP, noting the party has held the constituency since 2008 when it was known as the Senawang state seat before redelineation.
"We have won this state seat since 2008 when it was called the Senawang constituency. For four consecutive terms, we have continued to receive the support of the people here," he said.
Loke, who is also Negri Sembilan DAP chairman, thanked Gunasekaran for his excellent service to the residents and voters in the constituency.
The Seremban member of parliament said financial constraints faced by PH at the time did not stop Gunasekaran from serving and providing the best for the people.
"Gunasekaran is a true party man because in this state election, he is making way for a new face after winning four times in the seven elections he contested," Loke said.
Meanwhile, Mugunthan thanked the DAP leadership for trusting him to represent PH in Seremban Jaya.
"I will work hard to ensure the Seremban Jaya state seat remains with PH," said Mugunthan, who joined DAP in 2012.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor polls: PH strongholds at risk if Chinese voter turnout drops below 65%
Article:
JOHOR BARU: The turnout rate among Chinese voters could prove decisive in Pakatan Harapan's (PH) performance in the Johor election, with low participation threatening several of its traditional strongholds, according to Ilham Centre.
The research body said Barisan Nasional (BN) and PH were contesting on different battlegrounds, with BN relying on stable Malay support while PH depended heavily on urban and Chinese-majority constituencies.
Ilham Centre said BN had gained ground in Malay-majority areas following weakening confidence in Perikatan Nasional (PN) as an alternative political force.
"The uncertainty surrounding Pas and Bersatu's relationship, coupled with PN's unclear campaign narrative, has affected voter confidence," it said.
The analysis found that some Pas supporters were now more open to tactical voting for BN in selected Malay-majority seats, strengthening the coalition's position.
For PH, however, the main challenge was not a significant shift in support to BN but voter apathy among Chinese voters.
Ilham Centre said many Chinese voters remained politically aligned with PH but were less enthusiastic than they were during the 15th General Election (GE15), creating uncertainty over turnout.
The centre estimated PH needed Chinese voter turnout of at least 65 per cent to maintain its advantage in urban and semi-urban constituencies.
"A lower turnout could turn previously comfortable seats into competitive contests or even allow BN to make gains," it said.
Ilham Centre said it would be misguided to assume that voting patterns from GE15 would automatically be repeated in the Johor election, as state elections often produced different voter behaviour.
Campaigning for the 16th Johor election will officially end at 11.59pm today, in accordance with the Election Commission's (EC) election timetable.
Tomorrow, campaign rhetoric will give way to the voters' verdict as more than 2.7 million eligible Johoreans head to the ballot box to decide who will form the next Johor government.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: #NSTviral: Rat in food display sparks outrage, calls for action [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A viral video showing a rat roaming inside a restaurant's food display cabinet has sparked public outrage, with netizens calling for immediate action from the authorities.
The footage, shared on the social media platform X, shows the rodent crawling over trays of food believed to be prepared for customers.
The video has raised significant concerns over food hygiene, as rats are known to carry diseases and contaminate food through droppings, urine, and fur.
Social media users expressed shock and disgust, urging relevant authorities to inspect the premises and initiate enforcement action.
X user Shuaib Shahzaman claimed the area was already known for poor hygiene and urged Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) to intensify enforcement.
"That area is already dirty. DBKL needs to work twice as hard," he said.
Another user, Zamani Wangsa, said the video provided sufficient evidence for the public to lodge a formal complaint with the DBKL Health Department.
"With the available evidence, a report can be made to the DBKL Health Department," he said.
Meanwhile, user Jimm Yu sarcastically remarked on the size of the rat.
"The rat is so big. This place must be paradise for it."
